Block.Para: First Session, Thirty-seventh Parliament,

STATUTES OF CANADA 2002
CHAPTER 2
An Act respecting Sir John A. Macdonald Day and Sir Wilfrid Laurier Day
ASSENTED TO
21st MARCH, 2002
BILL S-14

Available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment designates the 11th day of January in each and every year as ГўВЂВњSir John A. Macdonald DayГўВЂВќ and the 20th day of November in each and every year as ГўВЂВњSir Wilfrid Laurier DayГўВЂВќ.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Sir John A. Macdonald Day and the Sir Wilfrid Laurier Day Act

Block.Para: 49-50-51 ELIZABETH II
ГўВЂВ”ГўВЂВ”ГўВЂВ”ГўВЂВ”ГўВЂВ”ГўВЂВ”
CHAPTER 2
An Act respecting Sir John A. Macdonald Day and Sir Wilfrid Laurier Day
[Assented to 21st March, 2002]
WHEREAS Sir John Alexander Macdonald was born on January 11, 1815;
AND WHEREAS he was one of the founders of Confederation;
AND WHEREAS he was the first Prime Minister of Canada;
AND WHEREAS Sir Wilfrid Laurier was born on November 20, 1841;
AND WHEREAS he was Prime Minister of Canada from 1896 to 1911;
AND WHEREAS he was the first Prime Minister of French ancestry and a fervent promoter of national unity;
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
Short Title
1. This Act may be cited as the Sir John A. Macdonald Day and the Sir Wilfrid Laurier Day Act.
Sir John A. Macdonald Day
2. Throughout Canada, in each and every year, the 11th day of January shall be known under the name of ГўВЂВњSir John A. Macdonald DayГўВЂВќ.
Sir Wilfrid Laurier Day
3. Throughout Canada, in each and every year, the twentieth day of November shall be known under the name of ГўВЂВњSir Wilfrid Laurier DayГўВЂВќ.
Published under authority of the Senate of Canada
Г‚В„
